strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Alabama is a prominent public flagship and R1 research university known for its spirited SEC culture, expansive Greek life, and highly aggressive merit scholarships. It serves as a major destination for high-achieving out-of-state students seeking a traditional college experience backed by generous financial incentives.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UA offers some of the most lucrative automatic merit scholarships in the country for out-of-state students with high standardized test scores.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants with a 32+ ACT or 1420+ SAT can automatically offset the vast majority of out-of-state tuition.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Greek life and SEC football dominate the social environment.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a vibrant, highly spirited, traditional campus culture will thrive, whereas those looking for a quiet, intimate liberal arts environment may find it overwhelming.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The Culverhouse College of Business and the Capstone College of Nursing are premier destinations with exceptional job placement and licensure pass rates.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants looking for strong ROI in business and healthcare are well-served, though nursing remains highly competitive.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and heavily influenced by SEC athletics (Crimson Tide) and a dominant Greek life system. Strong community support and numerous outdoor activities.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located on a large 1,970-acre campus in Tuscaloosa with adequate campus transit; highly residential.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: R1 institution with significant investments in research, highlighted by the new High Performance Computing and Data Center and the Shelby Institute.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional through Fall 2025; standard testing requirements are being reinstated for lower GPA brackets and completely reinstated for the Class of 2028.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist is rarely utilized for undergraduate admissions; reported at 0% admitted.

testing.policy_note: SAT submission rate 15%; ACT submission rate 43%.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking R1 research opportunities and robust co-op programs.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Computing proficiency required for passing core classes.

colleges.name: Culverhouse College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students targeting corporate, accounting, and finance roles across the Southeast.

majors.name: Accounting
majors.notes: Highly recruited by Big 4 accounting firms.

application_deadlines.notes: Priority deadline for holistic admissions and competitive scholarships.

supplemental_requirements.details: Select-A-Prompt short responses regarding leadership, extracurriculars, and adversity.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Strong emphasis on test scores for automatic merit scholarships.

major_selection_tactics: Apply early (before January 15) regardless of major to secure housing priorities and scholarship money.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying undeclared carries no admissions penalty, though certain programs like Nursing are competitive and best applied to directly.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate per semester up to 16 credits; additional fees apply for overload.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholar
scholarships.benefits: Awards $28,000 per year towards out-of-state tuition.

scholarships.name: UA Scholar
scholarships.benefits: Awards $24,000 per year towards out-of-state tuition.

scholarships.name: Foundation in Excellence
scholarships.benefits: Awards $15,000 per year towards out-of-state tuition.

scholarships.name: Presidential Elite
scholarships.benefits: Value of tuition for 4 years, first year of on-campus housing, $1,500 supplemental per year, $2,000 research allowance.

scholarships.name: UA Competitive Achievement Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Holistic award based on leadership and extracurricular excellence.

ap_policy.general_rule: Credit is awarded for scores of 3, 4, or 5 on most exams.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can be used towards core requirements but may not exceed 50% of the bachelor's degree requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit awarded for grades of 5 or higher on Higher Level exams.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum of 50% of the degree can be fulfilled by non-institutional credit.

what_it_takes: Early application submission for housing and scholarships
High standardized test scores (if aiming for lucrative out-of-state merit aid)
essay_tips: Detail specific leadership roles using the Select-A-Prompt options.
Quantify accomplishments and responsibilities in your activity descriptions.
red_flags: Applying late (missing the priority scholarship deadlines).
Poor math grades if applying to engineering or computer science.
insights: Out-of-state students heavily populate the campus, making for a diverse geographic mix despite the Southern culture.
The Honors College is highly accessible for those who meet the academic thresholds and provides significant registration and housing perks.
